# Contoso Brand Guidelines (extracted reference)

This file is generated from the brand-guidelines reference deck and is read by the case-study generator agent at runtime as design and voice guidance. It must never be copied into a generated customer-facing deck.

## Slide 9: CONTOSO LIMITED BRAND SYSTEM

- Brand Guidelines
- How to keep every new slide on-brand — logo, colour, type, motifs, voice and layout for anything you build beyond the templated pages.

## Slide 10: WHAT WE STAND FOR

- Brand foundation
- MISSION
- Help every customer build trust, prove value, and scale responsibly with technology that earns its place.
- VISION
- A world where enterprises adopt new capabilities with confidence, evidence, and control.
- BRAND PROMISE
- Build trust. Prove value. Scale responsibly.
- POSITIONING
- The evidence-led partner for enterprises modernising with care — practical, transparent, and outcome-driven.
- BRAND PERSONALITY
- Trustworthy
- Evidence-led
- Human
- Pragmatic
- Forward-looking

## Slide 11: IDENTITY

- The logo
- CONTOSO
LIMITED
- Primary lockup — monogram + wordmark
- MONOGRAM
- The “CL” disc. Use alone only where the full name already appears, e.g. avatars or favicons.
- CLEAR SPACE
- Keep clear space on all sides equal to the height of the CL monogram. Never crowd the lockup.
- MINIMUM SIZE
- Digital 120 px wide · print 25 mm wide. Below this, drop the wordmark and keep the monogram.
- COLOUR
- Amber monogram on navy; teal monogram on light. Wordmark navy on light, white on dark.

## Slide 12: IDENTITY

- Logo — what not to do
- ✕
- Don’t stretch or distort
- Keep the original proportions.
- ✕
- Don’t recolour
- Only palette colours, in the approved pairings.
- ✕
- Don’t rotate or tilt
- The lockup always sits level.
- ✕
- Don’t add effects
- No shadows, glows, bevels or outlines.
- ✕
- Don’t use low contrast
- Never place on busy or similar-tone backgrounds.
- ✕
- Don’t rearrange parts
- Never resize or reposition the monogram and wordmark.

## Slide 14: COLOUR

- Using colour & accessibility
- BALANCE  —  60 / 30 / 10
- 60%  Neutrals + navy
- 30%  Teal
- 10%
- Amber
- Let navy and light neutrals carry the page. Teal supports — panels, accents, one key figure. Amber is the spark: use it sparingly to draw the eye to a single element. Never split a slide evenly between all three.
- APPROVED TEXT PAIRINGS
- Aa
- White on Navy
- AAA
- PASS
- Aa
- Ink on White
- AAA
- PASS
- Aa
- Navy on Mist
- AAA
- PASS
- Aa
- Amber on Navy
- 7.4:1
- PASS
- Aa
- White on Teal
- 3.0:1
- LARGE ONLY
- Aa
- Amber / Teal text on white
- low
- AVOID

## Slide 15: TYPE

- Typography
- Aa
- APTOS DISPLAY · BOLD
- Aptos — the body typeface
- “Georgia italic sets the editorial pull-quote.”
- TYPE SCALE
- H1 · Slide title
- Aptos Display Bold
- 40 pt
- H2 · Section head
- Aptos Display Bold
- 24 pt
- Label / kicker
- Aptos Bold Caps, tracked
- 11–13 pt
- Body
- Aptos Regular
- 15–18 pt
- Caption / footer
- Aptos Bold
- 8–10 pt
- Pull-quote
- Georgia Italic
- 28–36 pt
- Fallbacks: if Aptos is unavailable use Segoe UI, then Calibri. Georgia → any serif.

## Slide 16: SYSTEM

- Graphic elements & motifs
- Numbered circles
- Steps, pillars and stages ride in solid discs, cycling navy → teal → amber. Bold number inside, caps label beneath.
- Split panels
- Openers and dividers pair a navy content field with a teal accent panel carrying geometric circles.
- “
- Quotation mark
- An oversized amber quote mark flags testimonials. Pair with generous whitespace — let one idea own the slide.

## Slide 17: STYLE

- Iconography & imagery
- ICONOGRAPHY
- △
- ◉
- ▢
- Single-weight, geometric line icons with rounded ends.
- Housed in a solid navy, teal or amber disc.
- One idea per icon — no gradients, no detail clutter.
- Monochrome fill; never mix palette colours inside one icon.
- PHOTOGRAPHY
- Authentic people at work in natural light. Uncluttered, real, and calm — cool neutral tones that sit beside navy and teal.
- DO
- Real workplaces & people
- Natural, bright light
- Generous negative space
- Candid, not posed
- DON’T
- Heavy filters or tints
- Busy backgrounds
- Clichéd stock metaphors
- Low-resolution images

## Slide 18: DATA

- Data visualisation
- FY22
- FY23
- FY24
- FY25
- Categorical colour order
- •
- Colour order
- Navy → Teal → Slate → Amber. Reserve amber to spotlight the one series that matters.
- •
- Keep it flat
- No 3D, no heavy gridlines. Use a single light divider line (#CAD7E3).
- •
- Label directly
- Put values on or beside the data. Drop legends where a direct label will do.
- •
- Cite the source
- Every chart carries a source line in the footer. Round numbers for readability.

## Slide 19: LANGUAGE

- Voice & tone
- Clear
- Plain words, short sentences, no jargon.
- WE SAY
- “Cuts onboarding to two weeks.”
- WE DON’T
- “Leverages synergistic paradigms.”
- Credible
- Evidence first — every claim backed by data or a source.
- WE SAY
- “Reduced tickets 32% (Q3 vs Q2).”
- WE DON’T
- “Massively reduces tickets.”
- Human
- Warm and direct. Talk to the reader.
- WE SAY
- “Here’s what changes for your team.”
- WE DON’T
- “The end-user shall observe changes.”
- Responsible
- Honest about limits; never overpromise.
- WE SAY
- “Results will vary by environment.”
- WE DON’T
- “Guaranteed results, every time.”

## Slide 20: MESSAGE

- Messaging & boilerplate
- Build trust.  Prove value.  Scale responsibly.
- THE TAGLINE — USE VERBATIM, IN THIS ORDER
- ELEVATOR PITCH
- Contoso Limited helps enterprises adopt new technology with confidence — pairing hands-on delivery with the evidence leaders need to scale.
- BOILERPLATE
- Contoso Limited is a synthetic demonstration brand used to showcase enterprise solution storytelling. It stands for building trust, proving value, and scaling responsibly. [Replace with the real organisation boilerplate.]
- NAMING
- Write “Contoso Limited” on first mention; “Contoso” thereafter. Never “Contoso Ltd.” in body copy.

## Slide 21: LAYOUT

- Layout & spacing
- Amber outline = safe margin
- CANVAS
- 16:9 widescreen — 13.33 in × 7.5 in (33.87 × 19.05 cm).
- SAFE MARGIN
- Keep content 0.55 in from every edge. Nothing critical outside it.
- LOGO LOCKUP
- Top-left, anchored at 0.55 in / 0.38 in on every slide.
- TITLE ZONE
- Left-aligned from 0.65 in, baseline near 1.1 in from the top.
- FOOTER
- Section tag bottom-left; two-digit page number bottom-right.
- GRID
- Think in a 12-column grid with ~0.2 in gutters; align to it.

## Slide 22: STEWARDSHIP

- Keeping the brand consistent
- BRAND STEWARD
- [Add owner name & role]
- QUESTIONS & APPROVALS
- [brand@contoso.example — add real contact]
- ASSET LIBRARY
- [Add link to the shared logo / template / image library]
- BEFORE YOU PUBLISH
- New slides on-brand? Check logo, palette, type, voice.
- BUILD TRUST.  PROVE VALUE.  SCALE RESPONSIBLY.
